visa_types.name: Cohabitation in Belgium
visa_types.documents_required:
To apply for a short term Schengen visa you will need multiple documents. Below are the requirements for Cohabitation in Belgium visa
Kindly click here to download checklist
Kindly click here to download language preference form
visa_types.visa_fees:
[bel > tza > en > Table:Business / Conference / Fair > Visa Fees 1]
Visa Service Fee: 108,000 TZS
Additional Services:
SMS 5,000 TZS
Photocopies 1,900 TZS
Courier 84,000 TZS
Form Filling Service 20,000 TZS
Prime Time Appointment 90,000 TZS
Applicants are requested to collect the invoice for all payments.
visa_types.processing_time:
Applications received at the Belgian Embassy/Consulate General will be processed within 2 and 15 working days, provided that a complete file is submitted. We recommend that you introduce your applications 15 days prior to the intended departure date.
Applications can be introduced up to 90 days in advance.
In case the file has to be submitted for decision to the Immigration Office in Brussels, the processing time may take more than 15 days.
Passports are kept at the Embassy for the duration of the processing of the visa application. Should you have an urgent need for your passport, please contact the VFS center where your application has been submitted or the Belgian Embassy/Consulate General via email to request a temporary withdrawal of your passport.
For more information on the processing time, please visit the website of the Belgian Ministry of Home Affairs (https://dofi.ibz.be/sites/dvzoe/FR/Apropos/Pages/Le_delai_de_traitement_d_une_demande_de_visa.aspx).
visa_types.overview:
Overview
If the purpose of is to cohabitate with your partner in Belgium you need to apply for Cohabitation Schengen visa. This service is available for residents who wish to apply for a Belgian Visa.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
If the purpose of is to cohabitate with your partner in Belgium you need to apply for Cohabitation Schengen visa. This service is available for residents who wish to apply for a Belgian Visa.

## Visa Fees

[bel > tza > en > Table:Business / Conference / Fair > Visa Fees 1]
Visa Service Fee: 108,000 TZS
Additional Services:
SMS 5,000 TZS
Photocopies 1,900 TZS
Courier 84,000 TZS
Form Filling Service 20,000 TZS
Prime Time Appointment 90,000 TZS
Applicants are requested to collect the invoice for all payments.

## Documents Required
To apply for a short term Schengen visa you will need multiple documents. Below are the requirements for Cohabitation in Belgium visa
Kindly click here to download checklist
Kindly click here to download language preference form

## Photo Specifications
Please  click here to get the new photograph requirements.

## Processing Time
Applications received at the Belgian Embassy/Consulate General will be processed within 2 and 15 working days, provided that a complete file is submitted. We recommend that you introduce your applications 15 days prior to the intended departure date.
Applications can be introduced up to 90 days in advance.
In case the file has to be submitted for decision to the Immigration Office in Brussels, the processing time may take more than 15 days.
Passports are kept at the Embassy for the duration of the processing of the visa application. Should you have an urgent need for your passport, please contact the VFS center where your application has been submitted or the Belgian Embassy/Consulate General via email to request a temporary withdrawal of your passport.
For more information on the processing time, please visit the website of the Belgian Ministry of Home Affairs (https://dofi.ibz.be/sites/dvzoe/FR/Apropos/Pages/Le_delai_de_traitement_d_une_demande_de_visa.aspx).

## Download Form
Please click here (https://visaonweb.diplomatie.be/Account/Login?ReturnUrl=/en) to fill in the online application form

visa_types.name: Family Reunion
visa_types.documents_required:
Family reunion - Art 10
Family reunion - Art 10bis
Family reunion with Belgian - Art 40ter
Language preference form
visa_types.visa_fees:
[bel > tza > en > Table:Study in Belgium > Visa Fees 1]
Visa Service Fee: 108,000 TZS
A visa administrative fee for long stay will have to be paid directly to the Belgian Immigration Office before the submission of your application.
You will find the information on the amounts and the payment method on this website. (https://dofi.ibz.be/fr/themes/faq/redevance)
Each applicant must present proof of full payment of the fee at the time of application. If this proof is not provided, the application is not eligible and will not be processed.
Additional Services:
SMS 5,000 TZS
Photocopies 1,900 TZS
Courier 84,000 TZS
Form Filling Service 20,000 TZS
Prime Time Appointment 90,000 TZS
Applicants are requested to collect the invoice for all payments.
visa_types.processing_time:
Don’t wait until the last moment to  submit your visa application 
The decision making time might be longer during some periods (summer months, holiday season). Therefore, make sure to submit your visa early enough.
Submit  a complete file 
Read attentively the information about the necessary documents to be provided before submitting your file. If the Consulate needs to ask for additional documents before being able to take a decision or before sending your file to the Immigration Office, you might not receive your visa on time.
For more information on the processing time, please visit the website of the Belgian Ministry of Home Affairs. (https://dofi.ibz.be/sites/dvzoe/FR/Apropos/Pages/Le_delai_de_traitement_d_une_demande_de_visa.aspx)
visa_types.overview:
Overview
Foreigners of third countries who want to join their family in Belgium.
For more information, please visit the website of the Immigration Office in Brussels: https://dofi.ibz.be/en/themes/third-country-nationals (https://dofi.ibz.be/en/themes/third-country-nationals)
Applicants applying for Belgium Long Stay Visa (D Visa) Category are requested to appear in person at the visa application center to register their biometrics which includes scans of all fingerprints and live digital photo.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Foreigners of third countries who want to join their family in Belgium.
For more information, please visit the website of the Immigration Office in Brussels: https://dofi.ibz.be/en/themes/third-country-nationals (https://dofi.ibz.be/en/themes/third-country-nationals)
Applicants applying for Belgium Long Stay Visa (D Visa) Category are requested to appear in person at the visa application center to register their biometrics which includes scans of all fingerprints and live digital photo.

## Visa Fees

[bel > tza > en > Table:Study in Belgium > Visa Fees 1]
Visa Service Fee: 108,000 TZS
A visa administrative fee for long stay will have to be paid directly to the Belgian Immigration Office before the submission of your application.
You will find the information on the amounts and the payment method on this website. (https://dofi.ibz.be/fr/themes/faq/redevance)
Each applicant must present proof of full payment of the fee at the time of application. If this proof is not provided, the application is not eligible and will not be processed.
Additional Services:
SMS 5,000 TZS
Photocopies 1,900 TZS
Courier 84,000 TZS
Form Filling Service 20,000 TZS
Prime Time Appointment 90,000 TZS
Applicants are requested to collect the invoice for all payments.

## Documents Required
Family reunion - Art 10
Family reunion - Art 10bis
Family reunion with Belgian - Art 40ter
Language preference form

## Photo Specifications
Please  click here to get the new photograph requirements.

## Processing Time
Don’t wait until the last moment to  submit your visa application 
The decision making time might be longer during some periods (summer months, holiday season). Therefore, make sure to submit your visa early enough.
Submit  a complete file 
Read attentively the information about the necessary documents to be provided before submitting your file. If the Consulate needs to ask for additional documents before being able to take a decision or before sending your file to the Immigration Office, you might not receive your visa on time.
For more information on the processing time, please visit the website of the Belgian Ministry of Home Affairs. (https://dofi.ibz.be/sites/dvzoe/FR/Apropos/Pages/Le_delai_de_traitement_d_une_demande_de_visa.aspx)

## Download Form
Please click here (https://visaonweb.diplomatie.be/Account/Login?ReturnUrl=%2Fen) to fill in the online application form

visa_types.name: Business / Conference / Fair
visa_types.documents_required:
To apply for a short term Schengen visa you will need multiple documents. Below are the requirements for Business / Conference / Fair visa
Kindly click here to download checklist
Kindly click here to download language preference form
Attention! Guarantees for return (assessment of migration risk): the applicant is requested to provide as much information as possible to document his/her family background, professional and socio-economic status, ownership of land or real estate (for instance: proof of family bonds with his/her country of origin, proof of regular income and stable source of revenues from the applicant and/or his/her partner, proof of property, etc.)
This information is essential to correctly judge the intention of the applicant to leave the Schengen area before the expiry of his/her visa.

In case the file has to be submitted for decision to the Immigration Office in Brussels, the processing time may take more than 15 days.
Passports are kept at the Embassy for the duration of the processing of the visa application. Should you have an urgent need for your passport, please contact the VFS center where your application has been submitted or the Belgian Embassy/Consulate General via email to request a temporary withdrawal of your passport.
For more information on the processing time, please visit the website of the Belgian Ministry of Home Affairs (https://dofi.ibz.be/sites/dvzoe/FR/Apropos/Pages/Le_delai_de_traitement_d_une_demande_de_visa.aspx).
visa_types.overview:
Overview
If the purpose of traveling is Business meetings, internal trainings, Conference or attending any trade fair. You need to apply for a Business / Conference / Fair short term Schengen visa. This service is available for residents who wish to apply for a Belgian Visa.
For more information, please visit the website of the Immigration Office in Brussels: https://dofi.ibz.be/en/themes/third-country-nationals (https://dofi.ibz.be/en/themes/third-country-nationals)

Should you have an urgent need for your passport, please contact the VFS center where your application has been submitted or the Belgian Embassy/Consulate General via email to request a temporary withdrawal of your passport.

visa_types.name: Short Stay to Settle in Belgium
visa_types.overview:
Short-Stay visa (C type) include all the categories of visa for a period of stay less than 90 days: Marriage in Belgium, Co-habitation in Belgium, Directive 2004-38-EC, etc.
- Marriage  in Belgium (#marriageinbelgium)
- Co-habitation  in Belgium (#cohabitationinbelgium)
- Directive  2004-38-EC (#directive2004-38-ec)
visa_types.full_text:
Short-Stay visa (C type) include all the categories of visa for a period of stay less than 90 days: Marriage in Belgium, Co-habitation in Belgium, Directive 2004-38-EC, etc.
- Marriage  in Belgium (#marriageinbelgium)
- Co-habitation  in Belgium (#cohabitationinbelgium)
- Directive  2004-38-EC (#directive2004-38-ec)

visa_types.overview:
Overview
- Foreign workers who wish to work in Belgium as employees (i.e. working under an employment contract) must hold a work permit, and the visa is needed accordingly.
- Foreign nationals wishing to settle in Belgium and be self-employed must hold a professional card authorising them to exercise their profession, and the visa is needed accordingly.
- For more information, please visit the website of the Immigration Office in Brussels:
https://dofi.ibz.be/en/themes/third-country-nationals (https://dofi.ibz.be/en/themes/third-country-nationals)
- Applicants applying for Belgium Long Stay Visa (D Visa) Category are requested to appear in person at the visa application center to register their biometrics which includes scans of all fingerprints and live digital photo.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
- Foreign workers who wish to work in Belgium as employees (i.e. working under an employment contract) must hold a work permit, and the visa is needed accordingly.
- Foreign nationals wishing to settle in Belgium and be self-employed must hold a professional card authorising them to exercise their profession, and the visa is needed accordingly.
- For more information, please visit the website of the Immigration Office in Brussels:
https://dofi.ibz.be/en/themes/third-country-nationals (https://dofi.ibz.be/en/themes/third-country-nationals)
- Applicants applying for Belgium Long Stay Visa (D Visa) Category are requested to appear in person at the visa application center to register their biometrics which includes scans of all fingerprints and live digital photo.

## Visa Fees

[bel > tza > en > Table:Study in Belgium > Visa Fees 1]
Visa Service Fee: 108,000 TZS
A visa administrative fee for long stay will have to be paid directly to the Belgian Immigration Office before the submission of your application.
You will find the information on the amounts and the payment method on this website. (https://dofi.ibz.be/fr/themes/faq/redevance)
Each applicant must present proof of full payment of the fee at the time of application. If this proof is not provided, the application is not eligible and will not be processed.

Photocopies 1,900 TZS

Applicants are requested to collect the invoice for all payments.

## Processing Time
Don’t wait until the last moment to  submit your visa application

Submit  a complete file 
Read attentively the information about the necessary documents to be provided before submitting your file. If the Consulate needs to ask for additional documents before being able to take a decision or before sending your file to the Immigration Office, you might not receive your visa on time.
For more information on the processing time, please visit the website of the Belgian Ministry of Home Affairs. (https://dofi.ibz.be/sites/dvzoe/FR/Apropos/Pages/Le_delai_de_traitement_d_une_demande_de_visa.aspx)

For more information on the processing time, please visit the website of the Belgian Ministry of Home Affairs (https://dofi.ibz.be/sites/dvzoe/FR/Apropos/Pages/Le_delai_de_traitement_d_une_demande_de_visa.aspx).
visa_types.overview:
Overview
If the purpose of traveling is for medical treatment in Belgium you need to apply for Airport Transit visa. This service is available for residents who wish to apply for a Belgian Visa.
For more information, please visit the website of the Immigration Office in Brussels: https://dofi.ibz.be/en/themes/third-country-nationals (https://dofi.ibz.be/en/themes/third-country-nationals)
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
If the purpose of traveling is for medical treatment in Belgium you need to apply for Airport Transit visa. This service is available for residents who wish to apply for a Belgian Visa.
For more information, please visit the website of the Immigration Office in Brussels: https://dofi.ibz.be/en/themes/third-country-nationals (https://dofi.ibz.be/en/themes/third-country-nationals)

## Visa Fees

[bel > tza > en > Table:Business / Conference / Fair > Visa Fees 1]
Visa Service Fee: 108,000 TZS
Additional Services:
SMS 5,000 TZS
Photocopies 1,900 TZS
Courier 84,000 TZS
Form Filling Service 20,000 TZS
Prime Time Appointment 90,000 TZS
Applicants are requested to collect the invoice for all payments.

## Documents Required
To apply for a short term Schengen visa you will need multiple documents. Below are the requirements for Airport transit visa
Kindly click here to download checklist.
Kindly click here to download language preference form
Attention! Guarantees for return (assessment of migration risk): the applicant is requested to provide as much information as possible to document his/her family background, professional and socio-economic status, ownership of land or real estate (for instance: proof of family bonds with his/her country of origin, proof of regular income and stable source of revenues from the applicant and/or his/her partner, proof of property, etc.)
This information is essential to correctly judge the intention of the applicant to leave the Schengen area before the expiry of his/her visa.

## Photo Specifications
Please  click here to get the new photograph requirements.

## Processing Time
Applications received at the Belgian Embassy/Consulate General will be processed within 2 and 15 working days, provided that a complete file is submitted. We recommend that you introduce your applications 15 days prior to the intended departure date.
Applications can be introduced up to 90 days in advance.
In case the file has to be submitted for decision to the Immigration Office in Brussels, the processing time may take more than 15 days.
Passports are kept at the Embassy for the duration of the processing of the visa application. Should you have an urgent need for your passport, please contact the VFS center where your application has been submitted or the Belgian Embassy/Consulate General via email to request a temporary withdrawal of your passport.
For more information on the processing time, please visit the website of the Belgian Ministry of Home Affairs (https://dofi.ibz.be/sites/dvzoe/FR/Apropos/Pages/Le_delai_de_traitement_d_une_demande_de_visa.aspx).

## Download Form
Please click here (https://visaonweb.diplomatie.be/Account/Login?ReturnUrl=/en) to fill in the online application form

Long-Stay visa (D type) include all the categories of visa for a period of stay over 90 days: study, work, family reunion, religious, re-entry, etc.

- Work in Belgium (#workinbelgium)
- Family Reunion (#familyreunion)
- Au pair (#aupair)
- Researcher (#researcher)

visa_types.name: Researcher
visa_types.documents_required:
Checklist for Researchers
Language preference form
visa_types.visa_fees:
[bel > tza > en > Table:Study in Belgium > Visa Fees 1]
Visa Service Fee: 108,000 TZS
A visa administrative fee for long stay will have to be paid directly to the Belgian Immigration Office before the submission of your application.
You will find the information on the amounts and the payment method on this website. (https://dofi.ibz.be/fr/themes/faq/redevance)
Each applicant must present proof of full payment of the fee at the time of application. If this proof is not provided, the application is not eligible and will not be processed.
Additional Services:
SMS 5,000 TZS
Photocopies 1,900 TZS
Courier 84,000 TZS
Form Filling Service 20,000 TZS
Prime Time Appointment 90,000 TZS
Applicants are requested to collect the invoice for all payments.
visa_types.processing_time:
Don’t wait until the last moment to  submit your visa application 
The decision making time might be longer during some periods (summer months, holiday season). Therefore, make sure to submit your visa early enough.
Submit  a complete file 
Read attentively the information about the necessary documents to be provided before submitting your file. If the Consulate needs to ask for additional documents before being able to take a decision or before sending your file to the Immigration Office, you might not receive your visa on time.
For more information on the processing time, please visit the website of the Belgian Ministry of Home Affairs. (https://dofi.ibz.be/sites/dvzoe/FR/Apropos/Pages/Le_delai_de_traitement_d_une_demande_de_visa.aspx)

For more information, please  visit the website of the Immigration Office in Brussels: https://dofi.ibz.be/en/themes/third-country-nationals (https://dofi.ibz.be/en/themes/third-country-nationals)
Applicants  applying for Belgium Long Stay Visa (D Visa) Category are requested to appear  in person at the visa application center to register their biometrics which  includes scans of all fingerprints and live digital photo.

visa_types.name: Short Stay
visa_types.overview:
Short-Stay visa (C type) entitles its holder to travel throughout the 26 Schengen States for up to 90 days in any 180-day period. Visas for visits exceeding that period remain subject to national procedures.Click here to know more about Schengen Visa. Guarantees for return (assessment of migration risk): The applicant is requested to provide as much information as possible to document his/her  family background, professional and socio-economic status, ownership of land or real estate (for instance : proof of family bonds with his/her  country of origin, proof of regular income and stable source of revenues from the applicant and/or his/her partner, proof of property, etc.) 
This information is essential to correctly judge the intention of the applicant to leave the Schengen area before the expiry of his/her visa.
In  case you have already submitted your fingerprints for a previous Schengen visa  application in the last 59 months, you are not required to submit them again.
However,  If you are uncertain whether your fingerprints have been captured for a  Schengen visa within the last 59 months, you are advised to appear in person.
Kindly  note that, if it would appear that the fingerprints prove not to be  present in the VIS system, you will be asked to reappear in person for new  biometric data collection. To avoid this possible complication, you have the  right to ask for your fingerprints to be taken again.
- Business / Conference / Fair (#business/conference/fair)
- Tourism (#tourism)
- Family and Friends Visit (#familyandfriendsvisit)
- Cultural and Sports Events (#culturalandsportsevents)
- ADS (#ads)
- Professional Training (#)
visa_types.full_text:
Short-Stay visa (C type) entitles its holder to travel throughout the 26 Schengen States for up to 90 days in any 180-day period. Visas for visits exceeding that period remain subject to national procedures.Click here to know more about Schengen Visa. Guarantees for return (assessment of migration risk): The applicant is requested to provide as much information as possible to document his/her  family background, professional and socio-economic status, ownership of land or real estate (for instance : proof of family bonds with his/her  country of origin, proof of regular income and stable source of revenues from the applicant and/or his/her partner, proof of property, etc.) 
This information is essential to correctly judge the intention of the applicant to leave the Schengen area before the expiry of his/her visa.
In  case you have already submitted your fingerprints for a previous Schengen visa  application in the last 59 months, you are not required to submit them again.
However,  If you are uncertain whether your fingerprints have been captured for a  Schengen visa within the last 59 months, you are advised to appear in person.
Kindly  note that, if it would appear that the fingerprints prove not to be  present in the VIS system, you will be asked to reappear in person for new  biometric data collection. To avoid this possible complication, you have the  right to ask for your fingerprints to be taken again.
- Business / Conference / Fair (#business/conference/fair)
- Tourism (#tourism)
- Family and Friends Visit (#familyandfriendsvisit)
- Cultural and Sports Events (#culturalandsportsevents)
- ADS (#ads)
- Professional Training (#)

visa_types.visa_fees:
[bel > tza > en > Table:Study in Belgium > Visa Fees 1]
Visa Service Fee: 108,000 TZS
A visa administrative fee for long stay will have to be paid directly to the Belgian Immigration Office before the submission of your application.
You will find the information on the amounts and the payment method on this website. (https://dofi.ibz.be/fr/themes/faq/redevance)
Each applicant must present proof of full payment of the fee at the time of application. If this proof is not provided, the application is not eligible and will not be processed.
Additional Services:
SMS 5,000 TZS
Photocopies 1,900 TZS
Courier 84,000 TZS
Form Filling Service 20,000 TZS
Prime Time Appointment 90,000 TZS
Applicants are requested to collect the invoice for all payments.
visa_types.processing_time:
Don’t wait until the last moment to  submit your visa application 
The decision making time might be longer during some periods (summer months, holiday season). Therefore, make sure to submit your visa early enough.
Submit  a complete file 
Read attentively the information about the necessary documents to be provided before submitting your file. If the Consulate needs to ask for additional documents before being able to take a decision or before sending your file to the Immigration Office, you might not receive your visa on time.
For more information on the processing time, please visit the website of the Belgian Ministry of Home Affairs. (https://dofi.ibz.be/sites/dvzoe/FR/Apropos/Pages/Le_delai_de_traitement_d_une_demande_de_visa.aspx)
visa_types.overview:

Foreigners wishing to study in Belgium need to apply for visas accordingly.
For more information, please visit the website of the Immigration Office in Brussels: https://dofi.ibz.be/fr/themes/faq/redevance (https://dofi.ibz.be/fr/themes/faq/redevance)
Applicants applying for Belgium Long Stay Visa (D Visa) Category are requested to appear in person at the visa application center to register their biometrics which includes scans of all fingerprints and live digital photo.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Foreigners wishing to study in Belgium need to apply for visas accordingly.
For more information, please visit the website of the Immigration Office in Brussels: https://dofi.ibz.be/fr/themes/faq/redevance (https://dofi.ibz.be/fr/themes/faq/redevance)
Applicants applying for Belgium Long Stay Visa (D Visa) Category are requested to appear in person at the visa application center to register their biometrics which includes scans of all fingerprints and live digital photo.

## Visa Fees

[bel > tza > en > Table:Study in Belgium > Visa Fees 1]
Visa Service Fee: 108,000 TZS
A visa administrative fee for long stay will have to be paid directly to the Belgian Immigration Office before the submission of your application.
You will find the information on the amounts and the payment method on this website. (https://dofi.ibz.be/fr/themes/faq/redevance)
Each applicant must present proof of full payment of the fee at the time of application. If this proof is not provided, the application is not eligible and will not be processed.
Additional Services:
SMS 5,000 TZS
Photocopies 1,900 TZS
Courier 84,000 TZS
Form Filling Service 20,000 TZS
Prime Time Appointment 90,000 TZS
Applicants are requested to collect the invoice for all payments.

## Documents Required
 Checklist for Students (PUBLIC School)
Checklist for Students (PRIVATE School)
Approved Medical Practitioners – Tanzania
Approved Medical Practitioners – Malawi
Approved Medical Practitioners – Mauritius
Approved Medical Practitioners – Zambia
Language preference form

## Photo Specifications
Please  click here to get the new photograph requirements.

## Processing Time
Don’t wait until the last moment to  submit your visa application 
The decision making time might be longer during some periods (summer months, holiday season). Therefore, make sure to submit your visa early enough.
Submit  a complete file 
Read attentively the information about the necessary documents to be provided before submitting your file. If the Consulate needs to ask for additional documents before being able to take a decision or before sending your file to the Immigration Office, you might not receive your visa on time.
For more information on the processing time, please visit the website of the Belgian Ministry of Home Affairs. (https://dofi.ibz.be/sites/dvzoe/FR/Apropos/Pages/Le_delai_de_traitement_d_une_demande_de_visa.aspx)

## Download Form

visa_types.visa_fees:
[bel > tza > en > Table:Business / Conference / Fair > Visa Fees 1]
Visa Service Fee: 108,000 TZS
Additional Services:
SMS 5,000 TZS
Photocopies 1,900 TZS
Courier 84,000 TZS
Form Filling Service 20,000 TZS
Prime Time Appointment 90,000 TZS
Applicants are requested to collect the invoice for all payments.
visa_types.processing_time:
Applications received at the Belgian Embassy/Consulate General will be processed within 2 and 15 working days, provided that a complete file is submitted. We recommend that you introduce your applications 15 days prior to the intended departure date.
Applications can be introduced up to 90 days in advance.
In case the file has to be submitted for decision to the Immigration Office in Brussels, the processing time may take more than 15 days.
Passports are kept at the Embassy for the duration of the processing of the visa application. Should you have an urgent need for your passport, please contact the VFS center where your application has been submitted or the Belgian Embassy/Consulate General via email to request a temporary withdrawal of your passport.
For more information on the processing time, please visit the website of the Belgian Ministry of Home Affairs (https://dofi.ibz.be/sites/dvzoe/FR/Apropos/Pages/Le_delai_de_traitement_d_une_demande_de_visa.aspx).
visa_types.overview:
Overview
If the purpose of traveling is to reside freely within the territory of Member States, you need to apply for Schengen visa accordingly. This service is available for residents who wish to apply for a Belgian Visa.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
If the purpose of traveling is to reside freely within the territory of Member States, you need to apply for Schengen visa accordingly. This service is available for residents who wish to apply for a Belgian Visa.

## Visa Fees

[bel > tza > en > Table:Business / Conference / Fair > Visa Fees 1]
Visa Service Fee: 108,000 TZS
Additional Services:
SMS 5,000 TZS
Photocopies 1,900 TZS
Courier 84,000 TZS
Form Filling Service 20,000 TZS
Prime Time Appointment 90,000 TZS
Applicants are requested to collect the invoice for all payments.

## Documents Required
To apply for a short term Schengen visa you will need multiple documents. Below are the requirements for DIRECTIVE 2004-38-EC  visa
Kindly click here to download checklist
Kindly click here to download language preference form

## Photo Specifications
Please  click here to get the new photograph requirements.

## Processing Time
Applications received at the Belgian Embassy/Consulate General will be processed within 2 and 15 working days, provided that a complete file is submitted. We recommend that you introduce your applications 15 days prior to the intended departure date.
Applications can be introduced up to 90 days in advance.
In case the file has to be submitted for decision to the Immigration Office in Brussels, the processing time may take more than 15 days.
Passports are kept at the Embassy for the duration of the processing of the visa application. Should you have an urgent need for your passport, please contact the VFS center where your application has been submitted or the Belgian Embassy/Consulate General via email to request a temporary withdrawal of your passport.
For more information on the processing time, please visit the website of the Belgian Ministry of Home Affairs (https://dofi.ibz.be/sites/dvzoe/FR/Apropos/Pages/Le_delai_de_traitement_d_une_demande_de_visa.aspx).

## Download Form
